name: CRUD/update
description: Instance update (PUT) conformance tests: version bump on content change vs. no bump on no-op updates, malformed Content-Type/If-Match rejection, client-supplied-id upsert-create, URL/body id-mismatch and missing-id rejection, and conditional-reference resolution via identifier (from UpdateTests).

variable.name: runId
variable.description: Runner-generated per-suite value used to isolate fixed-id create tests

variable.name: createdVersionId
variable.description: versionId read from ignixa-update-content after it was first PUT in setup

variable.name: versionAfterContentChange
variable.description: versionId read after the content-changing update in the first test

variable.name: versionAfterNoOp
variable.description: current versionId read after the no-op update; used for the subsequent matching If-Match precondition

variable.name: mismatchPatientId
variable.description: server-assigned id found by searching for the Patient created in setup for the URL/body id mismatch test

operation.description: PUT Patient/ignixa-update-content (Version A) to establish the resource for the content-change/no-op/etag tests
Read the setup Patient so later assertions do not depend on an optional update response body
POST a Patient with a server-assigned id, used later to demonstrate a URL/body id mismatch

assert.description: Setup create for mismatch Patient must return 201 Created

operation.description: Find the current-run mismatch Patient without depending on an optional create response body

assert.description: Setup search must return exactly one mismatch Patient entry

operation.description: PUT Practitioner/ignixa-update-prac1, referenced later via a conditional (identifier) reference

test.name: update with changed content assigns a new version and preserves the id
test.description: PUT Patient/ignixa-update-content with different content (Version B) must return 200 OK, keep the same id, and assign a new meta.versionId different from the one assigned at setup.

operation.description: PUT Patient/ignixa-update-content with Version B content and the observed current version
Read back the updated Patient because an update response body is optional

assert.description: meta.versionId must differ from the version assigned at setup

test.name: repeating an identical update does not create a new version
test.description: PUT Patient/ignixa-update-content again with the exact same content (Version B) must return 200 OK and must NOT bump meta.versionId, since there is no actual data change.

operation.description: PUT Patient/ignixa-update-content again with identical Version B content and the observed current version
Read back the Patient because an update response body is optional

assert.description: Informational: meta.versionId is unchanged when no data changed

test.name: update with unsupported Content-Type reports recommended 415 behavior
test.description: Informational MIME handling check: this request deliberately violates the client MIME requirement; 415 Unsupported Media Type is appropriate but not required as the exact server response by base R4.

operation.description: PUT Patient/ignixa-update-content with Content-Type: application/not-fhir+json

assert.description: Informational MIME handling check: 415 Unsupported Media Type is appropriate but not required by base R4

test.name: update with a client-supplied id for a non-existent resource performs an upsert-create
test.description: PUT Patient/ignixa-update-newpat-${runId}, which does not yet exist, must return 201 Created, assign the client-supplied id, and override the client-supplied meta.versionId ('9999') and lastUpdated with server-assigned values (FHIR R4 upsert-create semantics).

operation.description: PUT Patient/ignixa-update-newpat-${runId} with client-supplied meta.versionId=9999
Read back the upsert-created Patient because an update response body is optional

assert.description: id must match the client-supplied id
Server must have assigned a meta.versionId
Server must override the client-supplied meta.versionId of '9999'

test.name: update with a body containing no id returns 400
test.description: PUT Patient/ignixa-update-noid-target with a body that has no 'id' element at all must return 400 Bad Request (FHIR R4 requires the resource id to match the URL id).

operation.description: PUT Patient/ignixa-update-noid-target with a body lacking 'id'

test.name: update to a URL id that disagrees with the body id returns 400
test.description: Read back the Patient created in setup, then PUT that body (which has its own id) to a different URL id. The server must reject this with 400 Bad Request because the logical id in the URL and the body disagree (FHIR R4 §2.1.0.6).

operation.description: Read back the Patient created in setup to use its body (with its own id) as the update payload
PUT the read-back body (id=${mismatchPatientId}) to a deliberately different URL id

assert.description: Server must return 400 Bad Request when URL id and body id disagree

test.name: update with a correct If-Match version precondition succeeds
test.description: PUT Patient/ignixa-update-content with an If-Match header matching the resource's current meta.versionId must succeed with 200 OK.

operation.description: PUT Patient/ignixa-update-content with If-Match matching the current versionId

test.name: update with nonmatching If-Match entity-tags returns 412
test.description: PUT Patient/ignixa-update-content with the previously observed but now stale version must return 412 Precondition Failed.

operation.description: PUT Patient/ignixa-update-content using the initial version as a stale If-Match precondition

test.name: conditional reference by identifier on generalPractitioner resolves correctly
test.description: Informational server-behavior check: conditional-reference resolution is required in transactions, not for an ordinary PUT, so this test does not establish base-FHIR conformance.

operation.description: PUT Patient/ignixa-update-patgp1 with a conditional generalPractitioner reference

assert.description: Informational ordinary-PUT check: server should return a 2xx success status

operation.description: Read back the Patient because an update response body is optional

assert.description: Informational ordinary-PUT check: updated Patient should be readable
Informational ordinary-PUT check: generalPractitioner reference should resolve to the concrete Practitioner reference

operation.description: Teardown: delete the content-change/etag test Patient
Teardown: delete the upsert-create Patient ignixa-update-newpat-${runId}
Teardown: delete the mismatch-test Patient
Teardown: delete the generalPractitioner-reference Patient
Teardown: delete the referenced Practitioner
Teardown: defensively attempt to delete the no-id-body target in case a lenient server created it (expected to already be absent)
